message:
🐛 fix(agent): cache invalid OpenAI/Gemini 401, real minikube status, stale exec comment (#7923 #7984 #8002) (#8025)

Three small, independent backend fixes bundled into one review:

#7923 — validateOpenAIKey and validateGeminiKey re-fire on every startup.
Both returned (false, fmt.Errorf("invalid API key")) for 401 (and 403 for
Gemini), so ValidateAllKeys in server_operations.go:446-458 treated the
result as "network error — don't cache, retry later" and kept issuing live
/v1/models (or ListModels) requests on every kc-agent startup. Change both
to return (false, nil) to mirror validateClaudeKey, which already caches
invalid-key results correctly. Supersedes stalled external PR #7926
(same 2-line fix, blocked on DCO since 2026-04-13).

#7984 — Minikube profile status always "unknown" in the console.
listMinikubeClusters extracted profile names via regex but then hardcoded
Status: "unknown" for every profile. Added minikubeProfileStatus(name)
that shells out to `minikube status -p <name> -o json` (5s timeout per
profile) and parses either the single-object or the multi-node array form.
Returns "running" when every node reports Host=Running, "stopped"
otherwise, "unknown" only if the exec + JSON parse both fail. Timeout is
a named constant (minikubeStatusTimeout).

#8002 — Stale "silently dropped" comment on execMaxStdinBytes. The earlier
refactor added a WARN log at the drop site, so the constant's doc comment
claiming frames were "silently dropped" is no longer accurate. Updated to
note that a WARN log fires at the drop site. (The other half of #8002 —
rate-limiting the per-drop warn log — was already addressed in a previous
merge via per-session power-of-two throttling.)

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -23,7 +23,8 @@ import (
 const execAuthDeadline = 5 * time.Second
 
 // execMaxStdinBytes is the maximum allowed size of a single stdin message.
-// Messages exceeding this limit are silently dropped to prevent memory exhaustion.
+// Messages exceeding this limit are dropped (with a WARN log at the drop
+// site) to prevent memory exhaustion.
 const execMaxStdinBytes = 1 * 1024 * 1024 // 1 MB
 
 // execPingInterval is how often the server sends a WebSocket ping to detect dead peers.
